description: Pre-registered category registry for the agent-native Phase 0 suite. Freezes with the suite; no categories may be added, merged, or excluded after freeze (gates doc §0.3, §5).
gateRule: Phase 2a gate uses the pooled result of all gate-bearing wedge categories (W1+W2+W3), with no single category contributing more than 40% of pooled pairs (gates doc §4). Hardness/§3.6 pooling includes only pairs whose pair.json is gateEligible (pairs retired at ceiling never enter the pool). Exact per-category pair counts freeze here at suite freeze. Per-category results are reported but not individually gated.

categories.name: contract-preserving-refactors
categories.description: Refactor existing code without violating declared §Q/§S contracts; held-out tests probe contract-relevant behavior.

categories.name: contract-dense-algorithmic
categories.description: Implement or modify pure/contract-dense algorithmic code (Option/Result style) with contracts inside the modeled-forms whitelist.

categories.name: first-order-effects
categories.description: Changes crossing manifest-checked BCL effect boundaries (fs/net/db/console), first-order code only, no delegates. Calor's compile-error effect enforcement is sound here today.

categories.name: effect-safe-db-writes
categories.description: Deferred-eligibility wedge; pre-registered now so it cannot be invented post-hoc.

categories.name: heavy-bcl-ecosystem
categories.description: Tasks C# should dominate: dense ecosystem/library integration.

categories.name: debugger-dependent
categories.description: Diagnosis tasks that benefit from stepping through execution.

categories.name: neutral-bread-and-butter
categories.gateRole: regression-tolerance only (gates doc §4), never a Calor-advantage claim
categories.description: Ordinary bug fixes, small features, test writing. Guards against the wedge being purchased with general usability.
